seoTitle: Club Terrace vs Clyde — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Club Terrace and Clyde. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Club Terrace vs Clyde: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Club Terrace and Clyde.
heading: Club Terrace vs Clyde — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Club Terrace, Victoria and Clyde, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Clyde has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Club Terrace (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Club Terrace or Clyde safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Club Terrace has a flood risk score of 44/100, while Clyde scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Club Terrace scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. Clyde scores 49/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Club Terrace or Clyde?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Clyde has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
